title: Contact allergen review

presenting_history.text: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.
presenting_history.rationale: The UAE DHA guideline supports documenting exposure timing, suspected contact product or occupation, distribution, morphology, symptoms, recurrence, impact, and change without identifying an allergen automatically.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 1, 5, 6 and 9 — risks, morphology, distribution, symptoms, diagnosis, and history

red_flags.text: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.
red_flags.rationale: The red-flag section supports recording assessed mucosal, widespread, severe, infected, or systemic concern and clinician decisions without asserting those findings.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 7 and 13 — red flags and referral criteria

investigations.text: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.
investigations.rationale: Investigation and patch-test context was reviewed but no test is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Section 9 — patch testing, laboratory tests, photographs, and histopathology

source_limitations: Specific product and ingredient, exposure timing and dose, work tasks and protection, distribution, morphology, prior episodes, testing, confirmed allergen, diagnosis, treatment, workplace action, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
